2026-04-07T20:32:21.6216035Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-07T20:32:21.6216473Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-07T20:32:21.6216869Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-07T20:32:21.6217195Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

2026-04-07T20:34:34.4634461Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:32:55.845715246Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57883,\"title\":\"[AgentServer.Core] Docs: Document telemetry enrichment behavior and FoundryEnrichmentProcessor tag ordering\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit b8540535 (PR #57877) by `@needuv`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\n`FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` was updated to move `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` from `OnStart` to `OnEnd` so they take precedence over values set by underlying frameworks (e.g., OpenAI SDK) during a span\\u0026#39;s lifetime. The `microsoft.foundry.project.id` tag remains stamped in `OnStart`. These tags are driven by environment variables `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, and `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **README Telemetry section** mentions `AgentHostTelemetry` activity source names but says nothing about `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor`, the specific OTel tags it stamps (`gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, `gen_ai.agent.id`, `microsoft.foundry.project.id`), or the overwrite-resistance design rationale\\n- The **README FoundryEnvironment section** lists the environment variables it reads but does not explain how they flow through to OpenTelemetry span attributes\\n- The **CHANGELOG** (unreleased `1.0.0-beta.21`) has no entry for this behavioral change — agent identity tags now use `OnEnd` to prevent overwrite by underlying SDKs, which is a noteworthy behavioral guarantee for users correlating traces\\n- **No sample** covers the Foundry telemetry enrichment scenario (setting env vars and verifying OTel spans carry the expected agent identity tags)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`\\n\\nEdit (do not create). Find the existing `### Telemetry` subsection under `## Key concepts` and expand it to include the following content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Telemetry\\n\\nOpenTelemetry is configured automatically via `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore`. The `AgentHostTelemetry` class exposes the shared activity source and meter names (`ResponsesSourceName`, `InvocationsSourceName`, `ResponsesMeterName`, `InvocationsMeterName`) for the Responses and Invocations protocols. OTLP export is enabled when the `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` environment variable is set.\\n\\n#### Foundry span enrichment\\n\\nEvery span produced by the agent host is automatically enriched with Azure AI Foundry agent identity attributes by the `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor`. The attributes and the environment variables that drive them are:\\n\\n| OpenTelemetry attribute | Environment variable | Stamped in |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `microsoft.foundry.project.id` | `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID` | `OnStart` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.name` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME` | `OnEnd` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.version` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION` | `OnEnd` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.id` | `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}:{FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION}` (computed) | `OnEnd` |\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `gen_ai.agent.*` attributes are applied in `OnEnd` rather than `OnStart`. This ensures they overwrite any values that an underlying framework (such as the OpenAI .NET SDK) may have stamped during the span\\u0026#39;s lifetime, giving the agent host the authoritative last word on agent identity.\\n\\nSet the following environment variables before starting your agent server to have spans carry agent identity:\\n\\n```\\nexport FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME=\\u0026#34;my-agent\\u0026#34;\\nexport FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION=\\u0026#34;1.0.0\\u0026#34;\\nexport FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID=\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/.../resourceGroups/.../providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/my-workspace\\u0026#34;\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the unreleased `1.0.0-beta.21` section to add a `Bug Fixes` or `Features Added` entry describing the behavioral change:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` now stamps `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` in `OnEnd` instead of `OnStart`, ensuring agent identity attributes take precedence over values set by underlying frameworks (e.g., the OpenAI SDK) during a span\\u0026#39;s lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be stamped in `OnStart`.\\n```\\n\\nAdd this inside the existing `## 1.0.0-beta.21 (Unreleased)` block, in the `### Features Added` subsection (which already exists).\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new test-backed sample for Foundry telemetry enrichment.\\n\\n**Test file** (create): `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample3_FoundryTelemetry.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Diagnostics;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples;\\n\\npublic class Sample3_FoundryTelemetry\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    public void ConfigureFoundryTelemetryViaEnvironmentVariables()\\n    {\\n        #region Snippet:Core_Sample3_FoundryEnvVars\\n        // Set environment variables before starting the agent host to have every\\n        // OpenTelemetry span enriched with agent identity attributes.\\n        //\\n        // FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME   =\\u0026gt; gen_ai.agent.name\\n        // FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION =\\u0026gt; gen_ai.agent.version\\n        // FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME + \\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34; + FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION =\\u0026gt; gen_ai.agent.id  (computed)\\n        // FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID =\\u0026gt; microsoft.foundry.project.id\\n        //\\n        // These are typically set by the Azure AI Foundry platform when the agent\\n        // server is running as a hosted agent. You can also set them locally for\\n        // local development and integration testing.\\n        //\\n        // var builder = AgentHost.CreateBuilder();\\n        // // ... register protocols ...\\n        // var app = builder.Build();\\n        // app.Run();\\n        //\\n        // Spans emitted by the host will include:\\n        //   gen_ai.agent.name    = \\u0026#34;my-agent\\u0026#34;\\n        //   gen_ai.agent.version = \\u0026#34;1.0.0\\u0026#34;\\n        //   gen_ai.agent.id      = \\u0026#34;my-agent:1.0.0\\u0026#34;\\n        //   microsoft.foundry.project.id = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n        #endregion\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README placeholder** (inside `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/samples/` — create a new `Sample3_FoundryTelemetry.md`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Sample 3: Foundry Telemetry Enrichment\\n\\nThe agent host automatically stamps every OpenTelemetry span with Azure AI Foundry agent identity attributes when the `FOUNDRY_*` environment variables are set.\\n\\n## Environment variable to OTel attribute mapping\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already contains all required sections. Only the **Telemetry** subsection under **Key concepts** needs expansion as described above. No sections are missing.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders; check git diff to confirm\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24102615865/agentic_workflow) · ● 414.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57883\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-07T20:29:54Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-07T20:29:54Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-07T20:34:34.4694934Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:32:56.047865327Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57883,\"title\":\"[AgentServer.Core] Docs: Document telemetry enrichment behavior and FoundryEnrichmentProcessor tag ordering\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit b8540535 (PR #57877) by `@needuv`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\n`FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` was updated to move `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` from `OnStart` to `OnEnd` so they take precedence over values set by underlying frameworks (e.g., OpenAI SDK) during a span\\u0026#39;s lifetime. The `microsoft.foundry.project.id` tag remains stamped in `OnStart`. These tags are driven by environment variables `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, and `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID`.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **README Telemetry section** mentions `AgentHostTelemetry` activity source names but says nothing about `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor`, the specific OTel tags it stamps (`gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, `gen_ai.agent.id`, `microsoft.foundry.project.id`), or the overwrite-resistance design rationale\\n- The **README FoundryEnvironment section** lists the environment variables it reads but does not explain how they flow through to OpenTelemetry span attributes\\n- The **CHANGELOG** (unreleased `1.0.0-beta.21`) has no entry for this behavioral change — agent identity tags now use `OnEnd` to prevent overwrite by underlying SDKs, which is a noteworthy behavioral guarantee for users correlating traces\\n- **No sample** covers the Foundry telemetry enrichment scenario (setting env vars and verifying OTel spans carry the expected agent identity tags)\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`\\n\\nEdit (do not create). Find the existing `### Telemetry` subsection under `## Key concepts` and expand it to include the following content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Telemetry\\n\\nOpenTelemetry is configured automatically via `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore`. The `AgentHostTelemetry` class exposes the shared activity source and meter names (`ResponsesSourceName`, `InvocationsSourceName`, `ResponsesMeterName`, `InvocationsMeterName`) for the Responses and Invocations protocols. OTLP export is enabled when the `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` environment variable is set.\\n\\n#### Foundry span enrichment\\n\\nEvery span produced by the agent host is automatically enriched with Azure AI Foundry agent identity attributes by the `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor`. The attributes and the environment variables that drive them are:\\n\\n| OpenTelemetry attribute | Environment variable | Stamped in |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `microsoft.foundry.project.id` | `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID` | `OnStart` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.name` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME` | `OnEnd` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.version` | `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION` | `OnEnd` |\\n| `gen_ai.agent.id` | `{FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME}:{FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION}` (computed) | `OnEnd` |\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** `gen_ai.agent.*` attributes are applied in `OnEnd` rather than `OnStart`. This ensures they overwrite any values that an underlying framework (such as the OpenAI .NET SDK) may have stamped during the span\\u0026#39;s lifetime, giving the agent host the authoritative last word on agent identity.\\n\\nSet the following environment variables before starting your agent server to have spans carry agent identity:\\n\\n```\\nexport FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME=\\u0026#34;my-agent\\u0026#34;\\nexport FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION=\\u0026#34;1.0.0\\u0026#34;\\nexport FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID=\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/.../resourceGroups/.../providers/Microsoft.MachineLearningServices/workspaces/my-workspace\\u0026#34;\\n```\\n```\\n\\n#### File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the unreleased `1.0.0-beta.21` section to add a `Bug Fixes` or `Features Added` entry describing the behavioral change:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` now stamps `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` in `OnEnd` instead of `OnStart`, ensuring agent identity attributes take precedence over values set by underlying frameworks (e.g., the OpenAI SDK) during a span\\u0026#39;s lifetime. `microsoft.foundry.project.id` continues to be stamped in `OnStart`.\\n```\\n\\nAdd this inside the existing `## 1.0.0-beta.21 (Unreleased)` block, in the `### Features Added` subsection (which already exists).\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nAdd a new test-backed sample for Foundry telemetry enrichment.\\n\\n**Test file** (create): `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Samples/Sample3_FoundryTelemetry.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System.Diagnostics;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Tests.Samples;\\n\\npublic class Sample3_FoundryTelemetry\\n{\\n    [Test]\\n    public void ConfigureFoundryTelemetryViaEnvironmentVariables()\\n    {\\n        #region Snippet:Core_Sample3_FoundryEnvVars\\n        // Set environment variables before starting the agent host to have every\\n        // OpenTelemetry span enriched with agent identity attributes.\\n        //\\n        // FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME   =\\u0026gt; gen_ai.agent.name\\n        // FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION =\\u0026gt; gen_ai.agent.version\\n        // FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME + \\u0026#34;:\\u0026#34; + FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION =\\u0026gt; gen_ai.agent.id  (computed)\\n        // FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID =\\u0026gt; microsoft.foundry.project.id\\n        //\\n        // These are typically set by the Azure AI Foundry platform when the agent\\n        // server is running as a hosted agent. You can also set them locally for\\n        // local development and integration testing.\\n        //\\n        // var builder = AgentHost.CreateBuilder();\\n        // // ... register protocols ...\\n        // var app = builder.Build();\\n        // app.Run();\\n        //\\n        // Spans emitted by the host will include:\\n        //   gen_ai.agent.name    = \\u0026#34;my-agent\\u0026#34;\\n        //   gen_ai.agent.version = \\u0026#34;1.0.0\\u0026#34;\\n        //   gen_ai.agent.id      = \\u0026#34;my-agent:1.0.0\\u0026#34;\\n        //   microsoft.foundry.project.id = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;\\n        #endregion\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README placeholder** (inside `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/samples/` — create a new `Sample3_FoundryTelemetry.md`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Sample 3: Foundry Telemetry Enrichment\\n\\nThe agent host automatically stamps every OpenTelemetry span with Azure AI Foundry agent identity attributes when the `FOUNDRY_*` environment variables are set.\\n\\n## Environment variable to OTel attribute mapping\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already contains all required sections. Only the **Telemetry** subsection under **Key concepts** needs expansion as described above. No sections are missing.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders; check git diff to confirm\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24102615865/agentic_workflow) · ● 414.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57883\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-07T20:29:54Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-07T20:29:54Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-07T20:34:34.4812938Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:33:36.918157864Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":23,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"brandonh-msft\",\"id\":20270743,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/brandonh-msft\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1069434707,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention\",\"name\":\"Service
